{{Quote|It was always a when with Parja, never an if.|Fi Skirata|<ref name="Order 66: A Republic Commando Novel">''[[Order 66: A Republic Commando Novel]]''</ref>}}
'''Parja Bralor''' was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] who was the niece of former [[Cuy'val Dar]] [[Rav Bralor]]. She was a talented [[Engineer/Legends|engineer]] and loved to fix things, from a broken [[Chronometer/Legends|chrono]] to a sick [[Nuna/Legends|Nuna]] chick. She owned a successful workshop on [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]] and was eventually responsible for the maintenance of all the machinery at [[Kyrimorut/Legends|Kyrimorut]], the Skirata clan's home.

==Biography==
When [[Clone commando/Legends|clone commando]] [[Fi Skirata]] was brought to Mandalore following [[Brain/Legends|brain]] damage sustained at the [[Battle of Gaftikar|battle]] of [[Gaftikar]], she took on the job of nursing Fi back to health - caring for him, giving him physical therapy, and eventually falling in love with him. Parja was endlessly patient with Fi's loss of memory and coordination and was fiercely protective of him even after he regained some mobility and speech. Fi and Parja were married in a traditional Mandalorian wedding ceremony 998 days after the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|battle]] of [[Geonosis/Legends|Geonosis]] with Parja wearing her workshop overalls and Fi wearing [[Mandalorian armor/Legends|Mandalorian armor]]. The wedding - which took less than a minute - occurred right before Fi, now mostly recovered, left on a mission to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] with ARC trooper deserters [[Sull]], [[Spar]] and former [[Jedi General/Legends|Jedi General]] [[Bardan Jusik]].

When [[Clan Skirata]] deserted from the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] after [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]], she lived there with them in [[Kyrimorut/Legends|Kyrimorut]].

Parja wore armor of a deep scarlet color. Her chestnut braids were often tied with copper beads.
Her name is derived from the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] word "parjiir" meaning "victory".
